candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above operating profit, so it appears outside the selected COGS row 'Material and services'. However, it is a company-wide total and is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales, so assigning it specifically to COGS would be unsupported and risks misallocation/double counting.

Component of the total D&A disclosure, but not labelled as COGS-specific. Also subordinate to the broader 'Depreciation and amortisation' total, so it should not be selected separately when the total exists.
Granular amortisation component under the broader D&A disclosure, with no indication it belongs specifically to COGS.
Granular amortisation component likely tied to development/R&D rather than COGS; also subordinate to the broader D&A disclosure.
Granular amortisation component under total D&A, not identified as COGS-related.
Granular amortisation component under total D&A, not identified as COGS-related.
Appears to be a residual balancing line within the D&A block, not a clean COGS-specific D&A expense to add separately.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Material and services'. The statement shows D&A as a separate operating line, suggesting D&A is not embedded in that COGS row. But the disclosed D&A is a total operating amount, not specifically tied to COGS/cost of sales. Because the task requires only COGS-related D&A to add separately, no row can be safely assigned to COGS from this excerpt alone.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line above operating profit, so it appears outside the selected XSGA rows 'Selling, General & Administrative Expenses' and 'Research & Development Expense'. But it is a total operating D&A line and not labelled as SG&A/admin/overhead, so assigning it wholly to XSGA would be unsupported.

Could relate to development/R&D, but the statement already has a separate 'Research & Development Expense' row selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD. Without explicit evidence that this amortisation sits outside that R&D row and should be added to XSGA_DA, selecting it would risk distortion.
Component of the total D&A disclosure, not labelled as SG&A-specific, and should not be preferred over the summary total when the summary exists.
Granular amortisation component within the D&A block, but not identified as SG&A/admin-specific.

variables.notes: ADDED MANUALLY. Selected XSGA bucket is 'Selling, General & Administrative Expenses' plus 'Research & Development Expense', with XRD separately mapped to the same R&D row. The excerpt shows only a total operating D&A line and generic components, not SG&A- or R&D-specific D&A lines. Since no explicit SG&A/R&D D&A row is disclosed above operating profit, no row can be safely added separately to XSGA without unsupported allocation.
